_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates SW-Virginia batch, districts 42-46 (state tier). Jason Ballard (HD-42 R), Will Morefield (HD-43 R), Israel O'Quinn (HD-44 R), Terry Kilgore (HD-45 R), Mitchell Cornett (HD-46 R). Evidence-first finalization from primary roll-call records (LIS / VPAP), issue scorecards (ReproRising, Family Foundation, VCDL, Choice Tracker, ILA/limitedgov), and Cardinal News crossover reporting. ALL FIVE voted NAY on both marquee Jan-14-2026 amendments (HJ3 same-sex-marriage repeal, HJ1 reproductive-freedom) with the GOP bloc — the only 3 House-R crossovers on HJ3 were Bloxom/McNamara/Garrett, none of these five. NO contrary (FALSE) votes found for any of the five. Unproven dimensions left null per cardinal rule; freshman Cornett (sworn 2026-01-14) is genuinely thin.

profile.religion: Unconfirmed — sources conflict (a member biographical record lists 'Baptist'; local sources reference membership at First United Methodist Church, Pearisburg). Not asserted pending a clean primary source.
profile.background: VA Delegate, HD-42 (Giles/Pulaski/Montgomery, SW Virginia); first elected 2021 (old HD-12, defeating Chris Hurst), re-elected to redrawn HD-42 in 2023 and again Nov 4 2025. Trial attorney (Ballard Law LLC), U.S. Army JAG veteran (four combat deployments, Bronze Star, LtCol in the Army Reserve), married with three sons. Concord University; WVU College of Law (JD).

jason-ballard.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-42 (SW Virginia), sitting since 2021. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Conservative HARD record on the marquee 2026 votes: voted NAY on HJ1 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment, 1/14/2026) and NAY on HJ3 (same-sex-marriage repeal amendment, 1/14/2026) with the GOP bloc — NOT among the 3 Republican crossovers (Bloxom/McNamara/Garrett). ReproRising VA 2025 scorecard graded him 'F.' VCDL 2025 voting record: 100% pro-gun (84-0). No cross-party deviations and no contrary votes found. Six rubric dimensions (family/child sovereignty, christian liberty, election integrity, federal overreach, border/immigration, public justice) returned NO individually-cited evidence and are left null — not inferred from party. Religion is unresolved (Baptist vs United Methodist sources conflict).

0.note: Voted NAY on HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 64-34 party-line, 1/14/2026); ReproRising VA 2025 scorecard grade 'F' — consistent pro-life record.
Voted NAY on HJ3 (House 67-31, 1/14/2026), declining to repeal the constitutional definition of marriage as one man and one woman; not among the 3 GOP crossovers.

1.note: Voted NAY on the amendment that would enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

VCDL (Virginia Citizens Defense League) 2025 voting record scores Ballard 100% pro-gun (84 pro-gun / 0 anti-gun) — consistent opposition to red-flag/magazine-limit/'assault-weapon'/registry measures VCDL tracks.

profile.background: James W. 'Will' Morefield (R), VA House of Delegates, in office since 2010; represented HD-3 (2010-2024) and now HD-43 (Bland/Buchanan/Tazewell + parts of Dickenson/Russell, far SW Virginia) after redistricting; re-elected Nov 4 2025 with ~83%. Small-business / energy-services owner; B.A.S. Midwestern State University. Sits on Appropriations; Counties, Cities & Towns; and General Laws.

will-morefield.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-43 (far SW Virginia), sitting since 2010. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Conservative HARD record on the marquee 2026 votes: NAY on HJ1 (reproductive-freedom amendment), NAY on HJ3 (same-sex-marriage repeal), and NAY on HJ2 (felon voting-rights auto-restoration) — all 1/14/2026, with the GOP bloc, no crossover. ReproRising VA 2025 grade 'F.' 85% party-unity (VPAP). NUANCE for stewardship: Club for Growth (state) 2021 rated him the LOWEST Republican (69%), reflecting a coal-country pro-subsidy streak — not scored either way (no clean cell). VCDL placed him in its high pro-gun tier but an exact grade was not cleanly extractable, so self_defense left null. Christian-liberty, public-justice, federal-overreach, and border/immigration returned no individually-cited evidence and are left null — not inferred from party.

0.note: Voted NAY on HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (House 64-34, 1/14/2026); ReproRising VA 2025 grade 'F.'
Voted NAY on HJ3 (House 67-31, 1/14/2026), declining to repeal the one-man-one-woman constitutional definition; not among the 3 GOP crossovers.

1.note: Voted NAY on the amendment that would enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND — no denomination or church affiliation stated on Ballotpedia, Wikipedia, his campaign biography, or his legislative bio. Not asserted.
profile.background: Israel D. O'Quinn (R), VA House of Delegates since 2012 (HD-5, now HD-44: Bristol/Galax/Grayson + parts of Smyth & Washington, SW Virginia); re-elected Nov 4 2025; Deputy House Republican Leader. Businessman — Director of the Office of Strategic Initiatives at K-VA-T Food Stores (Food City); B.A. Emory & Henry College; prior service in AG Jerry Kilgore's office. Married to Emily O'Quinn.

israel-o'quinn.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-44 (SW Virginia), sitting since 2012; Deputy House Republican Leader. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Strong conservative HARD record: NAY on HJ1 (reproductive amendment) and NAY on HJ3 (same-sex-marriage repeal), 1/14/2026, with the GOP bloc (not among the 3 crossovers, per Cardinal News). The Family Foundation of Virginia 2022-23 Report Card scored him 100% (18/18), with documented '+' votes on Born-Alive Infant Protection, Informed Consent, Fairness in Female Sports, 'Sage's Law' (parental notification on gender identity), Parental Access to Minors' Medical Records, and Student Incident Reporting. No cross-party deviations or contrary votes found. Christian-liberty (specific bill), economic-stewardship, election-integrity, public-justice, federal-overreach, border/immigration, and self-defense (no roll call or grade located) returned no individually-cited evidence and are left null; religion unknown.

0.note: Family Foundation 100% card with '+' votes on Born-Alive Infant Protection and Informed Consent; voted NAY on HJ1 reproductive-freedom amendment (no GOP crossover reported on HJ1).
Voted NAY on HJ3 (House 67-31, 1/14/2026); Cardinal News names every GOP YES crossover (Bloxom/Garrett/McNamara) — O'Quinn not among them.

1.note: NAY on the amendment to enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Family Foundation '+' votes on Fairness in Female Sports and 'Sage's Law' — affirms biological sex / rejects transgender ideology in policy.

1.note: Family Foundation '+' votes on Parental Access to Minors' Medical Records and 'Sage's Law' (parental notification on a child's gender identity) — supports parental notification/consent.

profile.religion: Unconfirmed — a web snippet suggested 'Methodist' but no citable primary/secondary source was located. Not asserted.
profile.background: Terry G. Kilgore (R, b. 1961, Gate City/Scott County), attorney; VA House of Delegates since 1994; House Republican (Minority) Leader since June 2025; former Majority Leader 2022-2024; long-time Commerce & Labor chair. UVA-Wise (Clinch Valley); J.D. William & Mary. Twin brother Jerry Kilgore was VA Attorney General (2002-2005). Re-elected Nov 4 2025.

terry-kilgore.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-45 (far SW Virginia), sitting since 1994; House Republican Leader. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Veteran conservative HARD record: NAY on the reproductive-freedom amendment (party-line) and NAY on HJ3 same-sex-marriage repeal (House 67-31, 1/14/2026; not among the Bloxom/McNamara/Garrett crossovers). Sponsored HB304 born-alive infant protection; long anti-abortion vote list (Choice Tracker); Family Foundation 85%+; NRLC-endorsed. Led GOP opposition to the 2020 'assault-firearms' ban (HB961, called it an 'unconstitutional gun ban') and to Gov. Spanberger's 2026 assault-weapons/magazine amendments ('a de facto ban on common firearms'). Publicly opposed ending Virginia's ICE cooperation, calling it weakening enforcement and making the state 'less safe.' HONEST CAVEATS (not scored as TRUE/FALSE): on HJ3 he said 'the U.S. Supreme Court has spoken... there's nothing we should do about it' (voted NAY regardless); the ILA/limitedgov scorecard records anomalous YES votes on three firearm-restriction bills (HB2/HB183/SB100) that contradict his well-documented anti-gun-ban leadership and his min-wage/paid-leave YES votes drop his fiscal sub-score — treated as unresolved/artifact and left out of scoring. Election-integrity, family/child, christian-liberty, public-justice, federal-overreach left null.

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (party-line); sponsored HB304 born-alive infant protection; long anti-abortion vote record; Family Foundation 85%+ and NRLC-endorsed.

Voted NAY on HJ3 (House 67-31, 1/14/2026), declining to repeal the one-man-one-woman constitutional definition; not among the 3 GOP crossovers (despite a resigned public comment, his recorded vote was NAY).

1.note: NAY on the amendment to enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

Led GOP opposition to the 2020 assault-firearms ban (HB961, 'unconstitutional gun ban') and to Gov. Spanberger's 2026 assault-weapons/magazine amendments ('a de facto ban on common firearms'). (An anomalous ILA scorecard entry to the contrary is flagged in the notes as an unresolved artifact and not relied upon.)

2.note: Publicly and repeatedly opposed rescinding Virginia's ICE-cooperation order — 'isn't about compassion... it's about weakening immigration enforcement and making Virginia less safe' — i.e. opposes sanctuary/weakened-enforcement policy.

profile.religion: Christian (denomination not specified) — per his official House member record.
profile.background: Mitchell Cornett (R, b. 1994, Marion VA), Grayson County native; fifth-generation cattle farmer, licensed real-estate broker, Nationally Registered Paramedic / certified firefighter. Served on the Grayson County Board of Supervisors and as Vice-Chair of the county GOP committee. Won HD-46 (Grayson/Smyth/Wythe + part of Pulaski) Nov 2025 (~78%) succeeding Jed Arnold; sworn in Jan 14 2026. Married to Kaitlin Cornett.

mitchell-cornett.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-46 (SW Virginia); FRESHMAN sworn 2026-01-14 — record is genuinely thin (~3 weeks), so most cells are honestly null, not inferred. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] HARD: voted NAY on HJ3 same-sex-marriage repeal amendment (1/14/2026, House 67-31) — the conservative/party-line position. Chief patron of pro-law-enforcement bills HB294 (penalties for malicious injury to correctional officers), HB295 (correctional officers in aggravated-murder definition), HB1300 (survivor may buy a fallen State Police officer's service handgun for $1), and first-responder bills HB130/HB227. Campaign pledge 'Champion Life / protect the unborn'; campaign + VPM-interview statements as a 'staunch defender of the Second Amendment... without government overreach' ('Down here, we like our guns'). Economic signals (tax cuts, anti-VCEA, anti-APCO-rate-hike) don't map to a rubric cell and are left unscored. Family/child, christian-liberty, election-integrity, federal-overreach, and border/immigration: NO EVIDENCE (too new) — left null.

0.note: Campaign priority 'Champion Life': 'Every human life is precious, and I will always fight to protect life and advocate for... the unborn.' (SOFT — stated campaign position; freshman with no abortion roll call yet.)
Voted NAY on HJ3 (House 67-31, 1/14/2026), declining to repeal the one-man-one-woman constitutional definition.

1.note: NAY on the amendment to enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

0.note: Affirmative pro-law-enforcement advocacy: chief patron of HB294 (penalties for malicious injury to correctional officers) and HB1300 (survivor of a fallen State Police officer may purchase the service handgun for $1).

2.note: Chief patron of HB294/HB295 enhancing penalties for injuring/killing correctional officers — proportional punishment of evildoers.

1.note: Campaign 'Defend our Gun Rights': 'a staunch defender of the Second Amendment... without government overreach'; VPM interview 'Down here, we like our guns. We like our Second Amendment rights.' (SOFT — stated position; freshman with no gun roll call yet.)
